1d.notes: Very poor entry timing (lag=0.886); KAMA too slow at daily; wide band=74 but mostly slow configs; de-risked beta preserved (2022 -20.8% vs BH -71%)

4h.notes: Standard swing cadence; p05=-5.8 (weakest among all TFs); OOS=15.2% lower than 2h/1h; 2021 bull very strong (249%); reasonable cost drag (25 RT/yr)

2h.notes: WARN: very high turnover (395 RT/yr) from fast KAMA configs at 2h bar size (fast=6 bars); net already accounts for cost. 2022 bear worse than 1d/4h (-30.4%). Largest band (116)

1h.notes: Best move-capture profile (wtd_cap=0.302, lag=0.613); same OOS net as 2h (23.7%) but 4.4x lower turnover; best p05 among medium TFs (-2.2); 2022 bear suffers (-44.9%)

30m.notes: Similar capture to 1h (rc=0.271); smaller band=79; 2022 bear drops sharply to -54.6% (fine-TF bear-preservation failure); moderate cost drag vs 15m

15m.notes: Smallest band (47); very high turnover (505 RT/yr); KAMA whipsaws heavily at 15m; lowest 2020 bull participation (25.3%); capture no better than 30m despite 3x more trades

per_asset_note: Per-asset KAMA 4h OOS (u10, first 15 band configs): range -29% (AVAX) to +105.7% (BTC); 7/10 assets positive OOS. Per-asset TRAIN-top5 selection vs pooled band: mixed delta (-31pp LTC, +9pp BTC, others near zero) confirming prior finding that config-rank rho~0 does NOT transfer. The pooled KAMA band is safer than asset-specific band selection. Wide dispersion (range 135pp) persists at asset level -- systematic but not uniformly exploitable.
intraday_suitability: MODERATE. At 1h, KAMA reaches its best entry-lag (0.613) and capture (wtd=0.302), and OOS=23.7%. At 30m/15m the capture plateaus but turnover spikes (162-505 RT/yr) and bear preservation deteriorates sharply (-52 to -55% in 2022 vs -7.6% OOS maxDD). Fine-TF KAMA is high-cost, bear-fragile. Viable at 1h, marginal at 30m, risky at 15m.
longterm_suitability: WEAK at 1d due to extreme entry lag (0.886) and capture (rc=0.053). KAMA is an adaptive smoother designed to be FAST in trend; at daily cadence its adaptive speed becomes too slow to enter moves early. The 1d band ensemble preserves the 2022 bear (-20.8% vs BH -71%) but misses bull participation (2020 only 32.5%). Use 4h for long-term-ish accounts if bear-preservation is the objective, but accept poor move-catch economics.
verdict: KAMA is a SWING-to-INTRADAY type (sweet spot 4h-1h); 1h gives the best capture profile (entry-lag 0.613, wtd-capture 0.302, OOS 23.7%), while 4h is the lower-cost swing option (25 RT/yr). Daily KAMA has severe entry lag and is effectively a bear-hedge rather than a move-catcher. Fine TF (30m/15m) whipsaws heavily (500+ RT/yr) and loses bear preservation. All p05 values are negative (no TF passes the p05>0 ship gate), consistent with the universal LO de-risked-beta finding: KAMA is a drawdown-reducer not an alpha generator. KAMA's adaptive nature shines intraday (faster response than EMA in trends, slower in chop) -- measurable in the 1h capture improvement -- but cannot overcome the fundamental LO participate-preserve tradeoff.
